summary: UI freezes in the middle of writing for ca 5 secs, and then continues normally

description.text:
### Description

Installed via brew the latest version today.
As I'm working on a approx 5 pages / 1000 chars long text, I've experienced multiple times that the cursor/GUI freezes mid-writing, for maybe 5 seconds, and then continues normally.

Running on latest macos, 2020 M1 macbook pro. Mac: English language, light mode, connected to wifi, axternal screen (froze also as laptop only).

### Reproducing

By writing. I don't know how to recreate this as I don't see any clear reason for the freezing.

### Zettlr version

3.6.0

### Installation Method

Homebrew

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Tahoe 26.0.1

summary: feat: Arabic Edition - Comprehensive RTL Support and Arabic-Optimized Vim Mode

description.text:
## Overview

This PR introduces comprehensive Arabic language support for Zettlr, demonstrating what it takes to make Zettlr truly accessible to Arabic-speaking users. This fork serves as a **proof-of-concept and reference implementation** for RTL (Right-to-Left) language support in Zettlr.

**Important**: This proof-of-concept is based on the **v3.6.0 release tag** of Zettlr.

## Context

Zettlr currently lacks native RTL support, making it difficult for Arabic, Hebrew, Persian, and Urdu speakers to use the application effectively. This fork addresses this gap by providing a complete implementation that can serve as a foundation for upstream RTL support.

## Key Features

### 1. Complete Arabic UI Translation
- Full Arabic translation of all interface elements
- Native RTL layouts for menus, dialogs, and settings panels
- Proper text direction handling throughout the application

### 2. Fixed Keyboard Layout for Vim Mode
- Enables Vim navigation commands (h/j/k/l, etc.) to work with non-Latin keyboards
- No keyboard layout switching required
- Interactive **Keyboard Mapping Trainer** for modifier-based key combinations
- Essential for Arabic writers who use Vim bindings

### 3. Innovative Dual-Cursor System for Connected Scripts
- **Yellow word block**: Highlights entire connected Arabic word
- **Red character outline**: Precisely indicates character under cursor
- Preserves Arabic character connections while providing clear cursor positioning
- Respects Vim's visual feedback philosophy
- Handles partial Vim commands (like 'g' waiting for second character) without visual artifacts

**For detailed technical discussion of the cursor challenges for Arabic in Vim mode**, please see: [replit/codemirror-vim#248](https://github.com/replit/codemirror-vim/pull/248)

### 4. Robust RTL Text Handling in CodeMirror 6
- Proper cursor positioning for RTL text
- Context-aware script detection for mixed Latin/Arabic content
- Word boundary detection for connected scripts
- Full-height word block during partial commands to prevent text shifting

## Implementation Approach

Much of this work was developed with the assistance of Large Language Models (Claude/GPT), which enabled rapid prototyping and iteration. This approach demonstrates how AI-assisted development can accelerate the creation of complex features like RTL support and custom cursor systems.

**Note**: This is a **proof-of-concept fork** meant to:
- Demonstrate the feasibility of comprehensive RTL support in Zettlr
- Provide a reference implementation for upstream integration
- Showcase innovative solutions like the dual-cursor system for connected scripts
- Serve Arabic-speaking users immediately while upstream considers RTL support

## Screenshots

### Arabic Interface with Dual-Cursor Vim Mode
![Arabic Vim Mode](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/diraneyya/Zettlr-Arabic/refs/heads/v3.6.0-arabic/resources/screenshots/zettler_arabic_vim_mode.png)

*The dual-cursor system highlighting the connected Arabic word with yellow background and red character outline*

### Vim Settings with Keyboard Mapping Trainer
![Vim Settings](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/diraneyya/Zettlr-Arabic/refs/heads/v3.6.0-arabic/resources/screenshots/zettler_arabic_settings_vim.png)

*Interactive keyboard mapping trainer for non-Latin keyboard layouts*

## Technical Details

### Modified Components
- Complete Arabic translation (`static/lang/ar-AR.po`)
- RTL-aware UI components throughout Vue/Pinia layers
- Custom Vim plugin fork with dual-cursor system
- CodeMirror 6 extensions for script detection and word boundaries
- Configuration system for Vim keyboard mappings

### Architecture
- Context-aware cursor rendering based on script type (Latin vs Arabic)
- Multi-layer cursor DOM rendering (word block + character outline)
- Physical key mapping system for Vim commands
- Proper TypeScript types and peer dependencies for workspace integration

## Benefits for Upstream

This implementation provides:
1. **Ready-to-use Arabic translation** that can be integrated immediately
2. **RTL layout patterns** that can be applied to other RTL languages
3. **Innovative cursor solution** for connected scripts that could benefit Hebrew, Syriac, N'Ko users
4. **Vim keyboard fixes** that help all non-Latin keyboard users
5. **Proof that RTL support is achievable** without major architectural changes

## Compatibility

- ✅ Maintains full compatibility with upstream Zettlr
- ✅ No breaking changes to existing functionality
- ✅ All features are optional and user-configurable
- ✅ Works alongside existing LTR (Left-to-Right) languages

## Future Considerations

If this approach is adopted upstream, it could be extended to:
- Hebrew language support (RTL, non-connected script)
- Persian/Farsi support (RTL, connected script with different Unicode ranges)
- Urdu support (RTL, connected script similar to Arabic)
- Generalized RTL framework for all RTL languages

## Request for Feedback

We welcome feedback on:
1. Whether this approach aligns with Zettlr's architecture and goals
2. Which components could be integrated upstream vs maintained as fork
3. How to best support RTL languages in Zettlr's future development
4. Technical suggestions for improving the implementation

## Acknowledgments

- Original Zettlr team for creating this excellent Markdown editor
- CodeMirror team for the flexible editor framework
- Replit's CodeMirror Vim plugin as the foundation for cursor customization
- Claude AI (Anthropic) for development assistance

---

**Repository**: [github.com/diraneyya/Zettlr-Arabic](https://github.com/diraneyya/Zettlr-Arabic)  
**Branch**: `v3.6.0-arabic`  
**Base Version**: Zettlr 3.6.0 (release tag)

This fork demonstrates the feasibility and value of comprehensive RTL support in Zettlr as a proof-of-concept.

summary: Clicking on the folder in the file tree opens all subfolders in its full depth

description.text:
### Description

When opening a folder using a single click on the folder in the left sidebar, it unfolds the whole folder subtree to its full depth. It occures when clicking on the folder icon or name. Clicking on the little arrow left to the icon seems to work properly.

### Reproducing

In the left sidebar, click on arbitrary folder containing subfolders with files.

### Zettlr version

4.0.0-beta.2

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Smaller font size for footnotes inconsistently applied, and may not be an improvement in principle

description.text:
### Description

I don't think the small font size for footnote text is an improvement in the latest Beta: it makes footnotes a bit difficult to read on a small screen, and the CSS changes don't yet seem to have been consistently implemented. 

Footnotes now look a bit odd, as the scaling is not consistently applied: (a) citations appear in a larger font (the same size as in the main body of the text), and (b) the line spacing is too far apart (the line height for footnotes is set for the font size of the main body text). So I wondered if it would be better to default to footnotes having the same font size as body text (with users able to change this via the CSS)?

### Reproducing

Add a footnote containing a citation. 

### Zettlr version

Zettlr 4.0.0-Beta2

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

Zettlr 4.0.0-Beta2

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Sequoia 15.6.1

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: TOC not shown when file is first loaded

description.text:
### Description

The table of contents is not shown when a file is first loaded: it's updated only when the file is modified. I believe this is to avoid slowing Zettlr down - but it's still a bit annoying from the user perspective. It would be great if the TOC could be displayed immediately (maybe debouncing it to avoid slowing the app down). 

### Reproducing

Occurs whenever a file is opened for the first time. It's a known problem - as discussed here: https://discord.com/channels/609436111860793355/874677388053717052/1436004319307628676.

### Zettlr version

Zettlr 4.0.0.Beta1

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [ ] Windows
- [x] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [ ] Intel 64bit
- [x] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Sequioa 15.6.1

### Additional Information

_No response_

summary: Closing the last workspace or file fails to remove it from sidebar

description.text:
### Description

When the last file or workspace in the sidebar is closed, it remains visible in the sidebar. If it is a workspace, it will disappear if a file within is opened, at which point only the newly opened file will be visible.

### Reproducing

Remove the last workspace or file in the sidebar.

### Zettlr version

develop

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

summary: Zettlr abruptly scrolls to the top of the note while writing/editing

description.text:
### Description

While editing a note, sometimes (quite often in the beta version) Zettlr suddenly scrolls to the top of the note, requiring that you scroll back to where you were. 

### Reproducing

This does not happens all the time, but when it happens it happens repeatedly within seconds of scrolling back.

- Open a note.
- Go to a location in the node by scrolling down the text.
- Start editing/writing.
- Sometimes Zettlr suddenly jumps to the start of the note.
- Scroll back to the location where you were editing.
- Zettlr jumps back, potentially several times.

### Zettlr version

4.0 Beta 1

### Installation Method

From the Website or GitHub

### Installation Method (Other)

_No response_

### Your Platform

- [x] Windows
- [ ] macOS
- [ ] Linux

### Architecture

- [x] Intel 64bit
- [ ] ARM 64bit (includes Apple Silicon)

### Operating System Version

Windows 11

### Additional Information

_No response_
